name: Mashed Potato Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak bread slices in a deep bowl of milk or water for 15 minutes. Lift them out by the handful and squeeze the liquid out. Tear the bread into small pieces.
2. Peel the potatoes, cover with salted water, bring to a boil and cook for about 20 minutes. Drain. Add butter, salt, and hot milk, then mash until smooth.
3. Melt bacon grease in a large skillet, add onion and celery, and sauté until soft. Stir in the squeezed bread, then combine with mashed potatoes.
4. Add eggs, chopped celery leaves, parsley, and pepper.
5. Transfer mixture to a buttered casserole dish. Cover with foil and bake for 30 or 40 minutes at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Baste with turkey drippings if desired.
